highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 brings zero-allocation hot paths across HarfBuzzSharp and SKShaper, Skia m151 engine fixes, and new ReadOnlySpan overloads for SKColor.
highlights_body: Several hot-path allocations eliminated in text shaping (HarfBuzzSharp.Buffer.AddUtf8, Tag.Parse, SKFourByteTag.Parse, SKShaper glyph reads). The bundled Skia engine picks up upstream m151 bug fixes, and SKColor gains ReadOnlySpan<char> parsing overloads.

bullets.lead: Skia m151 upstream bug fixes merged
bullets.detail: Two successive upstream cherry-pick passes pull in Skia chrome/m151 engine corrections, keeping the bundled renderer current with upstream stability fixes.
SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char>, avoiding a string allocation when parsing hex colours from spans or stack-allocated buffers.

bullets.lead: GRContextOptions no longer requires an unsafe context
bullets.detail: The unnecessary unsafe modifier on GRContextOptions has been removed. Projects that interact with GPU context options no longer need to enable unsafe code for this type.
Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled encoder instead of allocating per call, and Tag.Parse no longer allocates a char[4] on every invocation — both reduce GC pressure in tight text-shaping loops.

bullets.lead: SKShaper glyph reads use zero-copy spans
bullets.detail: Glyph result data from the native shaper is now read directly via spans rather than intermediate copies, reducing per-run allocations in shaping pipelines.

bullets.lead: SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocates per call
bullets.detail: Eliminates a per-call char[4] heap allocation in SKFourByteTag.Parse, matching the same fix applied to the HarfBuzz Tag type.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Built the cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness, enabling pixel-level rendering comparisons across GPU backends
